title: Make state-root migration crash-recoverable
goal: Add a durable write-ahead and recovery contract for reviewed state-root migration and rollback so SIGKILL, host reboot or power loss between descriptor-relative effects and final receipt publication cannot leave an unclassified split state.

acceptance.assertion: Before the first rename, a create-only, fsync-bound journal records the exact reviewed plan, bound directory anchors, ordered effects and recovery authority; directory metadata durability is explicitly handled.
Every completed move is durably recorded before the next effect, without allowing a progress record to claim an effect that did not occur or omit an effect that did.
A read-only detector classifies incomplete journals after SIGKILL, reboot or power loss; a separate reviewed recovery action either completes or compensates only exact device/inode-bound effects.

Deterministic subprocess tests terminate the worker at every persistence and rename boundary for single-file, multi-entry and directory-tree cases and prove no false success, silent loss or deletion of foreign replacement objects.
The implementation documents and tests the required fsync, rename and directory durability semantics on supported local filesystems and fails closed on unsupported or unverifiable storage.
Validation uses temporary synthetic roots only; no active Bureau state migration, rollback, journal recovery or deletion is executed.

metadata.observation: T013 compensates caught exceptions using held descriptors and exact effect inodes, but SIGKILL, reboot or power loss can occur after one or more rename effects and before a final receipt exists.

risk_classification.impact: An abrupt stop can leave reviewed entries split between state root and quarantine without a durable machine-readable recovery ledger.
risk_classification.current_mitigation: Mutations remain explicit reviewed actions, ordinary exceptions compensate, receipts are create-only and no live migration was executed during T013.

lease_scope_migration.rationale: Crash recovery extends the state-root migration implementation and its durable recovery ledger.
does_not_establish: safe mutation of terminal historical tasks
